jokes.text: Why the fuck'd she call me?
jokes.setup: Larry gets called at 3am about someone's stroke whom he barely knows
jokes.punchline: His immediate self-centered confusion about why he's involved
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry David selfishness - turns someone else's medical emergency into personal inconvenience. Economy and character integration are strong.

jokes.text: What am I doing on that list of middle-of-the-night calls? I don't understand it. Better you than me. That's insane.
jokes.setup: Larry's confusion about being on emergency call list
jokes.punchline: Jeff's immediate selfish relief that he wasn't called instead
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing both Larry and Jeff's self-centeredness, but fairly standard for the show.

jokes.text: Now I'm on a text chain with his wife and the family... Those chains. I've been on 'em. They're the worst thing ever.
jokes.setup: Larry mentions being added to a family text chain
jokes.punchline: Jeff's immediate recognition and commiseration about how awful they are
jokes.explanation: Relatable modern annoyance that feels fresh for the show. Sets up a running theme that pays off throughout the episode.

jokes.text: You gotta heart that. Just heart it.
jokes.setup: Looking at a child's drawing sent to the text chain
jokes.punchline: Larry's reluctant acceptance that he must engage with performative digital empathy
jokes.explanation: Perfect capture of modern social media obligation. 'Heart it' becomes almost a command, showing Larry trapped in digital politeness.

jokes.text: Martha's beef stew is absolute perfection and really heals all wounds... It's really, honestly, like, the lowest form of human communication.
jokes.setup: Reading a typical saccharine text chain message about beef stew healing wounds
jokes.punchline: Larry's brutal assessment of it as 'lowest form of human communication'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from mild annoyance to philosophical condemnation. The juxtaposition of 'beef stew heals wounds' with 'lowest form of human communication' is perfectly Larry.

jokes.text: She's painting me as part of a series of old Jewish comedians called Wisenheimers.
jokes.setup: Larry has a date with an artist
jokes.punchline: The series title 'Wisenheimers' perfectly describes Larry's persona
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware humor. 'Wisenheimers' is a perfect description of Larry's character archetype.

jokes.text: Maybe he should've gone to Atlanta and given some water to someone in the voting line, maybe. Huh? Perhaps he should've given some water.
jokes.setup: Discussion of why Richard Lewis didn't get a second date with the artist
jokes.punchline: Larry's callback to previous season's Georgia voting water controversy
jokes.explanation: Callback works for fans but feels somewhat forced into this conversation. Not the strongest integration.

jokes.text: Because it's last minute, middle fucking seats.
jokes.setup: Susie bought plane tickets for textile convention
jokes.punchline: The added insult of middle seats on top of the unwanted trip
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - not only does he have to go, but middle seats make it worse. Sets up the dream scheme perfectly.

jokes.text: Just a little swing juice... I go with iced tea and a little bit of just vodka.
jokes.setup: Someone drinking from a flask on the golf course
jokes.punchline: Calling his vodka and iced tea concoction 'swing juice'
jokes.explanation: Clever euphemism that sounds both innocent and alcoholic. Good wordplay for a minor character.

jokes.text: You look like a derelict with that thing. You mean a gentleman. Here's looking up your address.
jokes.setup: Larry criticizes the flask drinking
jokes.punchline: Golfer's mangled version of 'here's looking at you' toast
jokes.explanation: Amusing malapropism that shows the character is already drunk. Minor but well-executed.

jokes.text: You have this dream, okay? And it's like a nightmare about the plane... It's a dream scheme.
jokes.setup: Larry devising a plan to get Jeff out of the trip
jokes.punchline: The alliterative name 'dream scheme' for the fake nightmare plan
jokes.explanation: Excellent Larry moment - devious plan with perfect naming. 'Dream scheme' is incredibly quotable and becomes the episode title. Shows Larry's manipulative creativity.

jokes.text: Middle seat! Can't breathe. Oh, they're squeezing me! Wake up! Wake up! Wake up!
jokes.setup: Larry demonstrating the fake nightmare performance
jokes.punchline: His over-the-top theatrical performance of airplane claustrophobia
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy as Larry rehearses his fake nightmare. His commitment to the bit is funny, though the performance itself is intentionally bad.

jokes.text: It's Picasso-esque. You know, a little asymmetrical... Asymmetrical?
jokes.setup: Artist describing Larry's face as interesting and asymmetrical
jokes.punchline: Larry's shocked reaction to being called asymmetrical
jokes.explanation: Good setup and reaction beat. Larry's vanity is wounded by the artistic compliment. His incredulous repetition works well.

jokes.text: The grimace looked like we were enjoying it?
jokes.setup: Waitress says it looked like they were enjoying the terrible pie
jokes.punchline: Larry's incredulous response about their obvious grimacing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry logic - pointing out the obvious absurdity of the waitress's misreading of their facial expressions.

jokes.text: It's halfway gone. You guys ate a lot of pie... went past the threshold of return ability.
jokes.setup: Manager explaining why they can't remove the pie from the bill
jokes.punchline: The concept of a 'threshold of return ability' for bad food
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd corporate logic. 'Threshold of return ability' sounds like real restaurant policy while being completely ridiculous.

jokes.text: You have to take a large bite. How else do you confirm? You can't confirm on a small bite. You don't get enough taste.
jokes.setup: Manager questioning why Larry took multiple bites of bad pie
jokes.punchline: Larry's logical explanation for needing large bites to properly confirm badness
jokes.explanation: Peak Larry logic - taking something absurd and making it sound completely reasonable. His food confirmation methodology is hilariously systematic.

jokes.text: She's an artist. She's a libertine. We're going back to my house. We're gonna have sex. We're gonna forget about this horrible experience.
jokes.setup: Restaurant argument reaching a peak
jokes.punchline: Larry's inappropriate oversharing about his sexual plans to win the argument
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation where Larry tries to assert dominance through TMI. 'Libertine' is a perfectly pretentious Larry word choice.

jokes.text: Yeah, go get Chef. You think I'm afraid of Chef?... Oh. Oh, I'm so scared of Chef.
jokes.setup: Manager threatens to get the chef
jokes.punchline: Larry's sarcastic bravado that will clearly backfire
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry overconfidence before inevitable disaster. His sarcasm sets up the quick exit perfectly.

jokes.text: You're gonna regret this choice. I'm the nice one of the two of us... Let's get the fuck out of here.
jokes.setup: Manager warns Larry about meeting the chef
jokes.punchline: Larry's immediate retreat after talking tough
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic cowardice. The immediate switch from bravado to flight is classic Larry. The threat works because we believe the chef would be intimidating.

jokes.text: What the hell? Larry, who the fuck is that? It's the housekeeper! Why didn't she knock? She's not supposed to be here! It's her day off!
jokes.setup: Housekeeper walks in during intimate moment
jokes.punchline: Panicked explanation while likely still in compromising position
jokes.explanation: Good cringe setup for the episode's main conflict. The multiple questions show their panicked state.

jokes.text: It's not like you don't have one of your own. It's...
jokes.setup: Trying to minimize the awkwardness with the housekeeper
jokes.punchline: Larry's tone-deaf attempt at anatomy-based logic
jokes.explanation: Peak Larry awkwardness - trying to logic his way out of embarrassment with the worst possible argument.

jokes.text: I know what you saw, and, uh... You know, I don't know the nature of your experiences, but, uh, a lot of women seem to find pleasure in that.
jokes.setup: Larry trying to explain the situation to his traumatized housekeeper
jokes.punchline: His clinical, educational approach to discussing oral sex
jokes.explanation: Incredible Larry moment - treating sex ed like a college lecture. The formal tone makes it infinitely more awkward.

jokes.text: I didn't wanna do it. I... She asked me. Oh, she practically begged me... What am I supposed to do? I didn't wanna be rude. You know, I'm a gentleman. It's a chivalrous thing to do.
jokes.setup: Larry continuing to explain himself
jokes.punchline: Reframing cunnilingus as chivalry and gentlemanly behavior
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant Larry logic - turning oral sex into a matter of politeness and chivalry. The comparison to holding doors is comedic genius and perfectly quotable.

jokes.text: You know, it's like a gentleman holds the door open for women, a gentleman puts the toilet seat down, and a gentleman, eh...
jokes.setup: Larry listing gentlemanly behaviors
jokes.punchline: The implied third item in the gentleman's code being oral sex
jokes.explanation: Peak Curb comedy - the escalating list of gentlemanly behaviors leading to the unspoken third item is perfect structure and timing. Completely quotable and referenceable.

jokes.text: Dahlia walked in on us when I was downtown... Downtown is fucking dangerous, you understand? You more of an uptown person.
jokes.setup: Larry using 'downtown' as euphemism for oral sex
jokes.punchline: Leon treating it as literal geography with safety concerns
jokes.explanation: Brilliant extended metaphor with Leon perfectly playing into the geography theme. The 'dangerous downtown' vs 'uptown person' distinction is comedically perfect.

jokes.text: Downtown is fucking dangerous, you understand? You more of an uptown person.
jokes.setup: Continuing the geographic sex metaphor
jokes.punchline: Leon's serious advice about sexual geography
jokes.explanation: Leon's character perfectly deployed - treating the metaphor literally with complete seriousness

jokes.text: I can fuck around and do a TED Talk to that shit. I'll have a pussy TED Talk. Get all the guys in one room, get a little microphone, get a little laser pointer and shit. Like, 'This is a pussy. See?'
jokes.setup: Leon establishing his expertise in the subject
jokes.punchline: The image of a formal TED Talk presentation about female anatomy with laser pointer
jokes.explanation: Leon's absurd confidence and the visual of a formal presentation with corporate tools makes this brilliantly ridiculous. Perfect Leon character moment.

jokes.text: All the eromenous zones... Erogenous. Eronenous zones. Erogenous. Erotenous. Erogenous zones.
jokes.setup: Leon trying to say 'erogenous zones'
jokes.punchline: The back and forth mispronunciations getting progressively worse
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Leon's confidence despite his malapropisms. The repetition works but isn't as strong as the TED Talk bit.

jokes.text: Claustrophobic. The middle seat. No room. No room! Ah! I can't breathe. Middle seat! Can't breathe!
jokes.setup: Jeff executing the dream scheme Larry taught him
jokes.punchline: His theatrical performance of the fake nightmare
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the dream scheme setup. Jeff's performance shows he learned from Larry's coaching, completing the callback structure.

jokes.text: If you're so worked up about this that you're having nightmares, it's a sign you should not come.
jokes.setup: Jeff's fake nightmare performance
jokes.punchline: Susie's reasonable response to what she thinks is genuine anxiety
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic payoff - the scheme works because Susie is actually being considerate, making Jeff and Larry's deception feel worse.

jokes.text: She brought it over just so you'd eat it and get fat? Here's the thing. Stu ate the entire tub himself.
jokes.setup: Gina blaming Susie for bringing foie gras
jokes.punchline: The revelation that Stu ate an entire tub of foie gras himself
jokes.explanation: Good escalation revealing the absurd cause of Stu's stroke. The image of eating an entire tub is both gross and funny.

jokes.text: Everybody has an Achilles' heel. An entire tub sounds like an Achilles' foot.
jokes.setup: Gina saying everyone has an Achilles heel
jokes.punchline: Larry's escalation to 'Achilles foot' for such a big weakness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay escalating from heel to foot. Perfect Larry wit with excellent economy - takes a cliché and makes it fresh.

jokes.text: Me, keep an eye on your 11-year-old daughter? You gotta be kidding. I don't know anything about children.
jokes.setup: Gina revealing Stu wants Larry to watch his daughter
jokes.punchline: Larry's horrified reaction to being asked to care for a child
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Larry's horror at child responsibility is completely believable and funny.

jokes.text: Anybody in the world. Go out in the stre... A homeless person would do a better job than me.
jokes.setup: Gina saying who better than Larry to help
jokes.punchline: Larry's immediate suggestion that literally anyone, including homeless people, would be better
jokes.explanation: Great self-deprecating Larry moment. The interrupted thought ('Go out in the stre...') adds to the desperation.

jokes.text: I don't like children. I've never spoken to a child without contempt in my voice in my life!
jokes.setup: Larry's continued protests about child care
jokes.punchline: The specific admission that he can't speak to children without contempt
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character insight - Larry's self-awareness about his contempt for children is both honest and funny. Very quotable line.

jokes.text: Olivia this week said she's now identifying as a cat, and I'm sure you could really help her with that, Larry.
jokes.setup: The child care responsibilities being discussed
jokes.punchline: The additional complication that the child now identifies as a cat
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the cat behavior that follows. The modern parenting element adds contemporary absurdity.

jokes.text: Well... Like what? Buy her a litter box? I mean, what am I supposed to do?
jokes.setup: Being told he can help with the cat identification
jokes.punchline: Larry's literal interpretation requiring a litter box
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry logic - taking the cat identification to its literal conclusion. Shows his practical confusion about modern parenting.

jokes.text: Freddy Funkhouser gets to sell a violin, and I get to take care of Catwoman?
jokes.setup: Learning about the different requests from Stu
jokes.punchline: The unfair comparison between valuable violin and difficult child care, plus 'Catwoman' nickname
jokes.explanation: Great comparison showing Larry's sense of injustice. 'Catwoman' is a perfect nickname that will stick.

jokes.text: Hello, pussycat.
jokes.setup: Larry meeting the cat-identifying child
jokes.punchline: His attempt at cat-appropriate greeting
jokes.explanation: Larry's awkward attempt to connect with the child's cat identity

jokes.text: Yeah, but there's something on the string. Hey! What are you doing? What was that? What'd you do that for? 'Cause cats like string.
jokes.setup: Olivia complimenting Larry's sweatshirt
jokes.punchline: She immediately pulls the string out because cats like string
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the cat setup - the logic is perfect from a child's perspective but destructive. Larry's shock is believable.

jokes.text: You can't wear a sweatshirt without the string. You ruined my sweatshirt, Mittens.
jokes.setup: The string being pulled out
jokes.punchline: Larry's exasperation and calling the child 'Mittens' instead of her name
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Larry losing his temper and the cat name 'Mittens' showing his inability to connect with children.

jokes.text: You cut the bottom of that bitch open and put the shit inside then reseal the bottom.
jokes.setup: Discussing the impossibility of putting toothpaste back in the tube
jokes.punchline: Leon's elaborate solution involving cutting and resealing the tube
jokes.explanation: Classic Leon logic - taking a metaphor literally and providing an unnecessarily complex solution. Shows his practical problem-solving approach.

jokes.text: You gotta moisten that motherfucker. Yeah. Like trying to thread a needle. Yeah, yeah, lick that shit good. You gotta get that bitch in that hole.
jokes.setup: Leon giving advice about threading the sweatshirt string
jokes.punchline: His increasingly sexual-sounding advice about the completely innocent task
jokes.explanation: Good Leon character moment - his crude language makes innocent activities sound inappropriate. The double entendre is unintentional on his part.

jokes.text: I like it from the top. Rolls out a lot easier... it's almost as if you're un-wiping your ass.
jokes.setup: Larry explaining toilet paper orientation preferences
jokes.punchline: The concept that bottom-rolling is like 'un-wiping your ass'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Larry observation turning toilet paper orientation into an anal hygiene issue. The 'un-wiping' concept is uniquely disgusting and memorable.

jokes.text: Getting caught eating pussy is not a basis... So, you fucked.
jokes.setup: Larry explaining why he can't fire the housekeeper
jokes.punchline: Leon's blunt summary and crude conclusion
jokes.explanation: Good Leon character moment - his crude directness cuts through Larry's elaborate explanations. The legal reasoning is actually sound.

jokes.text: Remember, after the rain comes the rainbow. Be the reason someone smiles today.
jokes.setup: Larry reading inspirational text chain messages
jokes.punchline: The clichéd, meaningless positivity that annoys him
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the text chain theme. The generic inspirational messages are realistically annoying.

jokes.text: Oy. That farbissina punim.
jokes.setup: Larry mentioning lunch with Gina
jokes.punchline: Susie's Yiddish insult meaning 'sour/bitter face'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Susie using authentic Yiddish. May not land with all audiences but fits her perfectly.

jokes.text: She wants you to be fat? Yes, she does. So I retaliated... Because she's jealous that I'm thinner than her and I have more style than her.
jokes.setup: Discussion of the foie gras situation
jokes.punchline: Susie's petty food war based on weight and style competition
jokes.explanation: Perfect Susie vanity and pettiness. The food war escalation over appearance competition is believably petty.

jokes.text: Because he's a fucking pig. It's not my problem.
jokes.setup: Larry pointing out Stu ate the whole tub and had a stroke
jokes.punchline: Susie's complete lack of sympathy or responsibility
jokes.explanation: Perfect Susie callousness. Her complete dismissal of responsibility while calling stroke victim a pig is brutal but in character.

jokes.text: He had a dream last night, Larry. A nightmare about the trip... I believe in respecting the unconscious.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Jeff isn't going on the trip
jokes.punchline: Susie's pseudo-psychological reasoning for respecting Jeff's fake dream
jokes.explanation: Great ironic payoff - Susie's genuine psychological concern for Jeff's fake nightmare. 'Respecting the unconscious' is perfectly pretentious.

jokes.text: Look at you. What a man will do for attention, huh?
jokes.setup: Larry visiting stroke victim Stu in the hospital
jokes.punchline: Larry's inappropriately casual joke about the stroke being attention-seeking
jokes.explanation: Peak Larry inappropriateness - treating a stroke as an attention-seeking behavior. Perfectly captures his social tone-deafness.

jokes.text: She invited me to lunch, and, uh, I paid, which is a little odd because she invited me. I'm the invitee. She's the inviter. Usually, the inviter pays.
jokes.setup: Larry talking to stroke victim about lunch with his wife
jokes.punchline: His detailed complaint about payment etiquette to a man having a medical crisis
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Larry moment - complaining about lunch etiquette to a stroke victim. The 'invitee/inviter' terminology shows his systematic approach to grievances.

jokes.text: She pulled the string on my sweatshirt, and it was all I could do to restrain myself from yelling, 'You fuck!' That's what I wanted to say to your daughter.
jokes.setup: Larry explaining his interaction with Stu's daughter
jokes.punchline: His admission of wanting to curse at an 11-year-old, told to her hospitalized father
jokes.explanation: Incredible Larry moment - his complete honesty about wanting to curse at a child, told to the child's stroke-victim father. Peak inappropriate timing and content.

jokes.text: Freddy Funkhouser, who loves prepubescent girls. And I mean that in a good way. He's very excited to be around them. And, of course, again, I mean that in a good way.
jokes.setup: Larry suggesting Freddy would be better with kids
jokes.punchline: His repeated clarifications making innocent statement sound worse with each 'good way' disclaimer
jokes.explanation: Brilliant cringe comedy - Larry's attempts to clarify make it worse. Each 'good way' makes it sound more suspicious. Perfect example of digging yourself deeper.

jokes.text: Will you look at the tuchus on this one? Hey, you know what? Is she coming in here? She giving you sponge baths, this one? Huh?
jokes.setup: Attractive nurse enters stroke victim's room
jokes.punchline: Larry's inappropriate sexual commentary to the hospitalized patient
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry inappropriateness - sexual comments in a hospital room. 'Tuchus' adds Yiddish flavor while being crude.

jokes.text: Maybe you're right, Larr. Freddy's the right person for Olivia. And you're... you're the right person for the violin. So, yeah, yeah, you wanna flop us?
jokes.setup: Larry's inappropriate behavior in the hospital
jokes.punchline: Stu's decision to switch assignments, clearly motivated by Larry's behavior
jokes.explanation: Good callback and payoff - Larry's inappropriate behavior gets him what he wanted but through the wrong means. Stu's 'flop' terminology is perfect.

jokes.text: Because I was holding the towels when you and her were... They remind me of the trauma.
jokes.setup: Larry asking why there are no towels
jokes.punchline: Dahlia's explanation that towels trigger her trauma from witnessing the sex act
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the trauma theme. The specific detail about holding towels makes the trauma more ridiculous and specific.

jokes.text: Well, those are my towels... Oh, so it's just my towels?
jokes.setup: Larry asking about towels at her own home
jokes.punchline: The revelation that she can use her own towels but not Larry's
jokes.explanation: Great absurd logic - the trauma is specific to Larry's towels but not towels in general. Shows the selectivity of her 'trauma.'

jokes.text: I caught my aunt giving my Uncle Moishe a blow job in the back of a Pontiac, but if they asked me to go to the movies, I just hopped in the car. Same back seat!
jokes.setup: Arguing that seeing sex shouldn't affect work performance
jokes.punchline: Larry's personal anecdote about witnessing relatives and still using the same car
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Larry personal revelation. The specific details (Uncle Moishe, Pontiac, same back seat) make it vivid and quotable. Perfect example of Larry's inappropriate oversharing.

jokes.text: I did have a crush on my aunt, though, after that.
jokes.setup: The story about catching his aunt and uncle
jokes.punchline: The inappropriate admission about developing romantic feelings for his aunt
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry inappropriate revelation. The casual admission of incestuous feelings delivered matter-of-factly is peak Larry awkwardness.

jokes.text: Columbus got it from the Indians, brought it back to Spain with grapefruit.
jokes.setup: Discussion about who invented cunnilingus
jokes.punchline: Larry's absurd historical theory involving Columbus and grapefruit
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd historical revisionism. The pairing with grapefruit adds perfect random specificity. Classic Larry pseudo-intellectual theorizing.

jokes.text: Put that motherfucker on... on the Mount Rushmore of... of pussy eaters. They all got their tongue out and shit. You know what I mean?
jokes.setup: Discussion of honoring cunnilingus pioneers
jokes.punchline: Leon's vision of a Mount Rushmore with tongues sticking out
jokes.explanation: Incredible visual comedy from Leon. The image of Mount Rushmore with tongues out is perfectly absurd and memorable. Peak Leon creativity.

jokes.text: You took something elegant, something classy and sophisticated like my flask, and you shoved it up my ass, and you reamed me with it.
jokes.setup: Freddy confronting Larry about the violin scheme
jokes.punchline: His elaborate sexual metaphor for being betrayed using his own flask
jokes.explanation: Great Freddy outrage with vivid sexual metaphor. The contrast between 'elegant and sophisticated' flask and crude betrayal imagery works well.

jokes.text: I'd rather have roaches in my bathtub as pets than take care of a kid!
jokes.setup: Larry protesting about having to take care of Olivia
jokes.punchline: His extreme comparison preferring roach pets to child care
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Larry hyperbole. The specific image of bathtub roach pets is perfectly disgusting and shows his extreme aversion to children.

jokes.text: My humor's way too sophisticated for a child!... Nobody's said a decent word about me my entire life! I don't even say decent words about me!
jokes.setup: Freddy and Larry arguing about their respective qualities
jokes.punchline: Larry's self-deprecating admission that even he doesn't compliment himself
jokes.explanation: Great Larry self-awareness moment. His admission of never receiving or giving himself compliments is both sad and funny.

jokes.text: I'm gonna roll over and do some fucking myself. That guy thinks I'm an alcoholic!
jokes.setup: Freddy threatening revenge against Larry
jokes.punchline: His concern about Stu thinking he's an alcoholic because of the flask use
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the flask issue. Freddy's wounded pride about being seen as alcoholic adds another layer to his grievance.

jokes.text: Not in a bad way, but they connect. They sit and laugh, and, you know, it's a little push, a little pull, and... Not in a bad way, but they connect.
jokes.setup: Freddy continuing to describe Larry with children
jokes.punchline: His repeated 'not in a bad way' disclaimers making innocent interaction sound worse
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Larry's earlier disclaimers. Freddy making the same mistake shows how these attempts at clarification backfire.

jokes.text: You don't need to finish high school. You need to pay your dues at the mattress store in Temecula... And take off that skirt, you look like a whore.
jokes.setup: Freddy having a nightmare about child care
jokes.punchline: His dream version of abusive parenting advice
jokes.explanation: Good payoff showing Freddy's subconscious fears about parenting. The specific details (mattress store in Temecula) add absurd specificity.

jokes.text: You shit in the bucket, you clean the bucket. Those are the house rules. Who cares if it's your birthday? We go to cockfights. That's what this family does.
jokes.setup: Freddy's nightmare continuing
jokes.punchline: Increasingly abusive and bizarre parenting scenarios
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the nightmare. Cockfights as family tradition is absurdly specific and dark.

jokes.text: You're using the dream scheme on the wrong guy. I invented it.
jokes.setup: Dahlia trying to use trauma nightmares as excuse
jokes.punchline: Larry revealing he invented the fake nightmare technique
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback and payoff line. Larry's pride in inventing the scheme and catching someone else using it is quintessential Larry. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: You think you're too good for the chain? You know what? Waylan? Is that your name? I do. I think I'm too good for the chain.
jokes.setup: Waylan confronting Larry about not participating in text chain
jokes.punchline: Larry's honest admission of superiority complex about the chain
jokes.explanation: Great Larry honesty moment. His direct admission of thinking he's too good is both arrogant and refreshingly honest.

jokes.text: Every two minutes it's, 'Ding!' I'm getting a ding. I'm getting a ding. Too many dings! And what is it? It's just these ridiculous, inspirational sentiments that amount to nothing.
jokes.setup: Larry explaining his text chain frustrations
jokes.punchline: His mounting frustration with the constant notifications and meaningless content
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Larry's text chain frustration. 'Too many dings' is relatable and his critique of inspirational content is sharp.

jokes.text: There's no nightmares! It's a nightmare con! And I know, 'cause I invented it!
jokes.setup: Larry discovering Freddy used the dream scheme
jokes.punchline: His outraged revelation that it's all fake, citing his invention of the technique
jokes.explanation: Great callback payoff with Larry's outrage at his own scheme being used against him. 'Nightmare con' is perfect terminology.

jokes.text: I don't want either one of you taking care of anything!
jokes.setup: Larry and Freddy fighting over the violin in her house
jokes.punchline: Gina's exasperated decision to revoke both assignments
jokes.explanation: Good resolution to the assignment switching. Both Larry and Freddy lose, which feels like appropriate cosmic justice.

jokes.text: You would've been a hell of a father... Social workers would've descended on the house within two weeks.
jokes.setup: Leon complimenting Larry's potential parenting
jokes.punchline: Larry's immediate self-awareness about child protective services
jokes.explanation: Great Larry self-awareness. The specific timeframe ('two weeks') and 'descended' verb choice make it funnier.

jokes.text: Hey, listen. About the other day, I know it was really awkward, but it's not even really my thing. You know? It's Larry's thing. He really likes that.
jokes.setup: Renee returning to drop off Larry's painting
jokes.punchline: Her awkward attempt to distance herself from the sexual act
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing the lasting awkwardness. Her attempt to make it Larry's fault/preference is relatable but cowardly.
